goal: Decks and Profile pages sat at ~440px mobile width on desktop with huge empty space. Add additive >=1024px desktop layouts matching the .mtd-* system.
done: DECKS (css/decks.css): widened .page-shell.decks-shell to 1080px, deck heading bigger, .decks-grid -> 4 columns (was 2), bigger cells/hover lift, .decks-shop-nudge capped to a centred 720px band. PROFILE (css/profile.css): turned the single column into a 2-col dashboard via grid on .section-block.profile-redesign (300px left rail = #profile-identity + #profile-streak; right = #profile-tabs + active .profile-tab panel + #profile-cta) with explicit grid-row placement; shell widened to 940px. Pure CSS, additive @media(min-width:1024px) only. TH (th/decks.html, th/profile.html) inherit automatically — selectors key off body[data-page] and the th/ pages load the same shared CSS. Tab show/hide JS untouched (hidden panels stay display:none).
verified: Headless render at 1440px: decks now a 4-col grid + large heading + centred nudge (sparse only because guest has 1 unlocked deck); profile now a 2-col dashboard (identity+streak rail left, Rewards/Activity/Settings tabs + Achievements + Daily + lifetime-stats band right). Mobile <1024px unchanged (rules are inside the media query).
